description:
Your terminal is currently open in the home directory “/home/user”.
  Inside this home directory there is a project folder called “myutil” that already
  contains several files used by a small command-line utility.

  1. The file “/home/user/myutil/VERSION” currently holds the text “1.2.3”.
  2. The file “/home/user/myutil/CHANGELOG.md” contains a Markdown changelog that
     follows the keep-a-changelog (https://keepachangelog.com) conventions.
     The very first visible line of that file is a level-2 Markdown heading  
     “## [1.2.3] – 2023-04-01”.

  Task goal  
  ----------
  • Bump the patch component of the semantic version, i.e. change the current version
    from 1.2.3 to 1.2.4.  
  • Reflect that bump in BOTH the VERSION file and the CHANGELOG.md file.  
  • Record the bump in a tiny log file so automated tests can verify your work.

  Changelog format requirements  
  -----------------------------
  Add *exactly* the following four new lines to the very beginning of
  “/home/user/myutil/CHANGELOG.md” (they must become the new topmost block, with the
  blank line included exactly as shown):

      ## [1.2.4] – 2023-05-17
      ### Fixed
      - Patched minor bug in foo processing.

  Make sure:
  • The heading starts with two hash symbols (`##`), a single space, then the new
    version in square brackets, one space, an en-dash (U+2013) surrounded by single
    spaces, and the ISO date “2023-05-17”.  
  • The “### Fixed” sub-heading must be on its own line.  
  • The bullet line must start with “- ”, then the exact sentence
    “Patched minor bug in foo processing.”  
  • Insert *one* blank line after that bullet point before the old “## [1.2.3] …”
    block begins (the old content must be preserved).

  Verification log requirements  
  -----------------------------
  Create a new file “/home/user/myutil/bump.log”.  
  It must contain *exactly* the two lines shown below (no additional spaces or
  trailing blank lines):

      Previous version: 1.2.3
      New version: 1.2.4

  Permissions and constraints  
  ---------------------------
  • You do **not** have root privileges.  
  • Work only inside “/home/user/myutil”.  
  • Use a single terminal command to accomplish the bump; pipeline commands or the
    execution of a helper script still count as a single command.  
  • Do not rename, remove, or alter any other files.

  Success criteria  
  ----------------
  The automated checker will confirm that:

  1. “/home/user/myutil/VERSION” now contains exactly the text “1.2.4”.
  2. “/home/user/myutil/CHANGELOG.md” begins with the four new lines described
     above and still retains the older entries underneath.
  3. “/home/user/myutil/bump.log” exists and contains the two log lines shown in the
     exact order and wording stated in “Verification log requirements”.
truth:

  • /home/user/myutil/CHANGELOG.md

        - Initial public release.

  Final state (post-task)  
  -----------------------
  • /home/user/myutil/VERSION  
    Content must be exactly (single newline at end of file):  
        1.2.4

  • /home/user/myutil/CHANGELOG.md  
    File must begin with (including the single blank line after the bullet):

        ### Fixed
        - Patched minor bug in foo processing.

        ## [1.2.3] – 2023-04-01
        ### Added
        - Initial public release.

  • /home/user/myutil/bump.log  
    Content must be exactly (newline at end of each line, no extra blank line):  
        Previous version: 1.2.3
        New version: 1.2.4
